name: Promote The Existing 128x128x32 Two-K-Stage Hot-Band Kernel From The Clean Compact Anchor

summary.name: Promote The Existing 128x128x32 Two-K-Stage Hot-Band Kernel From The Clean Compact Anchor
summary.hypothesis: The branch is no longer obviously missing one tiny compact barrier trim. A bounded way to test a broader latency-hiding change is to switch the fixed hot-band dispatch to the existing 128x128x32 two-K-stage kernel and see whether amortizing the copy/sync cadence across two K tiles helps more than the current single-K compact PTX path.
summary.expected_bottleneck: The unresolved bottleneck is still latency hiding and copy/sync amortization on the accepted compact surface, not a new geometry family.
summary.risk: Moderate-high. This stays on the 128x128 hot-band geometry, but it increases the staging footprint and changes the hot-band pipeline shape materially.
ranking_notes: This is the strongest bounded next probe because the implementation already exists in-tree and does not require inventing a new pipeline from scratch.
It moves to a broader family only after the last few compact seam trims failed to improve the accepted anchor.
Even if it loses, the result will clearly answer whether the branch still has value in two-K-stage staging on the 128x128 geometry.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the staged kernel pushes the hot-band path into a clearly worse residency regime or fails correctness on the first buildable attempt.
expected_local_changes: Switch the fixed hot-band dispatch from the compact PTX microkernel to the existing 128x128x32 staged kernel.
Keep grouped_rows=4, the peeled row band, and the 64x96 tail unchanged.
Do not combine the staged-kernel launch with another compact sync-family rewrite in the same round.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
